questions.q: Subrahmanyan Chandrasekhar is best known for his work in which field of science?
questions.explanation: Chandrasekhar was a theoretical astrophysicist renowned for his work on the structure and evolution of stars.

questions.q: What major award did Chandrasekhar win in 1983?
questions.explanation: Chandrasekhar shared the 1983 Nobel Prize in Physics for his theoretical studies of the structure and evolution of stars.

questions.q: The limit named after Chandrasekhar applies to which kind of star?
questions.explanation: The Chandrasekhar limit is the maximum mass a stable white dwarf star can have.

questions.q: Approximately how many solar masses is the Chandrasekhar limit?
questions.explanation: A white dwarf above roughly 1.4 times the mass of the Sun cannot support itself and collapses.

questions.q: In which country was Chandrasekhar born?
questions.explanation: Chandrasekhar was born in Lahore in British India in 1910.

questions.q: Chandrasekhar was the nephew of which earlier Indian Nobel laureate?
questions.explanation: C. V. Raman, winner of the 1930 Nobel Prize in Physics, was Chandrasekhar's uncle.

questions.q: With which American university was Chandrasekhar associated for most of his career?
questions.explanation: Chandrasekhar spent the bulk of his career at the University of Chicago.

questions.q: By what short name was Chandrasekhar widely known to colleagues?
questions.explanation: He was affectionately known as 'Chandra,' and NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory is named after him.

questions.q: What pressure prevents a white dwarf below the Chandrasekhar limit from collapsing?
questions.explanation: Electron degeneracy pressure, a quantum effect, supports white dwarfs against gravitational collapse.

questions.q: A NASA space observatory named after Chandrasekhar observes the universe in what kind of light?
questions.explanation: The Chandra X-ray Observatory, launched in 1999, studies the universe in X-rays.

questions.q: In what year was Chandrasekhar born?
questions.explanation: Chandrasekhar was born on 19 October 1910 in Lahore.

questions.q: Chandrasekhar reportedly made his key insight about white dwarf mass during which journey?
options: A sea voyage from India to England
A flight to the United States
questions.explanation: He worked out the mass limit as a young man during his 1930 ship voyage from India to Cambridge.

questions.q: Which famous astronomer publicly ridiculed Chandrasekhar's collapse theory in the 1930s?
questions.explanation: Sir Arthur Eddington dismissed Chandrasekhar's result, a clash that delayed acceptance of the theory for years.

questions.q: At which English university did Chandrasekhar earn his PhD?
questions.explanation: Chandrasekhar completed his doctorate at Trinity College, Cambridge, in 1933.

questions.q: Chandrasekhar's Nobel Prize work showed that a star above the limit must end as which of these?
options: A neutron star or black hole
questions.explanation: Above the limit, electron degeneracy fails and the remnant collapses into a neutron star or black hole.

questions.q: Chandrasekhar served for nearly two decades as managing editor of which leading journal?
questions.explanation: He edited The Astrophysical Journal from 1952 to 1971, shaping it into a premier publication.

questions.q: With whom did Chandrasekhar share the 1983 Nobel Prize in Physics?
questions.explanation: He shared the prize with William A. Fowler, who was honored for work on the formation of chemical elements.

questions.q: Which late-career book by Chandrasekhar examined a topic in classical literature alongside science?
options: Newton's Principia for the Common Reader
questions.explanation: His last book, 'Newton's Principia for the Common Reader,' reflected his lifelong study of Newton's work.

questions.q: The physics underlying the Chandrasekhar limit combines gravity with which area of physics?
questions.explanation: The limit arises from applying quantum statistics and special relativity to a degenerate electron gas.

questions.q: Chandrasekhar's wide-ranging research included a major treatise on which fluid topic?
questions.explanation: His book 'Hydrodynamic and Hydromagnetic Stability' (1961) became a classic reference in the field.

questions.q: In which city was Chandrasekhar born, then part of British India?
questions.explanation: He was born in Lahore, which is now in Pakistan.

questions.q: Chandrasekhar's discovery was important for understanding which type of stellar explosion?
questions.explanation: A white dwarf reaching the Chandrasekhar mass triggers a Type Ia supernova, used as a cosmic distance marker.

questions.q: Chandrasekhar's mass limit depends on a property of the star's composition called the:
questions.explanation: The limit scales inversely with the square of the mean molecular weight per electron of the stellar material.

questions.q: In which year did Chandrasekhar first publish his result on the maximum mass of white dwarfs?
questions.explanation: He published the mass-limit result in 1931, shortly after arriving in Cambridge.

questions.q: Chandrasekhar's 1939 book that became a foundational text was titled:
options: An Introduction to the Study of Stellar Structure
The Mathematical Theory of Black Holes
questions.explanation: 'An Introduction to the Study of Stellar Structure' (1939) consolidated his early work on stellar interiors.

questions.q: Late in his career, Chandrasekhar wrote an influential 1983 monograph on which exotic objects?
questions.explanation: 'The Mathematical Theory of Black Holes' (1983) was a landmark study of black hole physics.

questions.q: The CFS instability of rotating stars carries Chandrasekhar's name alongside Schutz and which other physicist?
questions.explanation: The Chandrasekhar-Friedman-Schutz (CFS) instability describes how rotating stars can become unstable through gravitational radiation.

questions.q: Which highest U.S. civilian science honor did Chandrasekhar receive in 1966?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the U.S. National Medal of Science in 1966 (he also later received the Copley Medal in 1984).

questions.q: Chandrasekhar's early stellar work treated the electron gas in a white dwarf as which kind of gas at high density?
questions.explanation: He recognized that at high density the degenerate electrons become relativistic, which is what produces a finite mass limit.

questions.q: In what year did Chandrasekhar pass away?
questions.explanation: Chandrasekhar died in Chicago in 1995 at the age of 84.
